name: prison_labor_pen_contract_state_procurement_ban
premise: State procurement director Casey Rivera must cancel or renew a below-market ballpoint pen contract produced in state prison workshops after a disability-rights group shows ergonomic injury clusters and subminimum wage structures violate new fair-chance procurement rules. Warden Opal Grant argues cancellation idles vocational programs that reduce violence and fund victim restitution pools. Renewal preserves cheap supplies for schools; cancellation may spike costs during budget sequestration.

agents.name: Casey Rivera
agents.identity: I am the state procurement director enforcing fair labor clauses, disability accommodations in contracted workplaces, and cost controls for education agencies.
agents.goal: I want ethical supply chains without shredding rehabilitative programs that currently finance restitution.

agents.name: Opal Grant
agents.identity: I am the prison warden who runs the workshop program and who documents violence metrics tied to idle time when contracts vanish abruptly.
agents.goal: I want a phased wind-down with replacement vocational tech training funded before cancellation, even if that delays reform headlines.

choices: I will cancel the contract immediately and emergency-source pens from unionized private vendors with wage-floor certifications, accepting school supply price spikes this semester.
I will renew for one fiscal year with mandated ergonomic retrofits, minimum wage mirroring state floors, and third-party injury audits, accepting legal exposure if audits find coercion patterns.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Auditor general testimony starts at eight a.m. Casey Rivera must choose between immediate cancellation with private sourcing or a one-year renewal with wage and ergonomics mandates. What does Casey Rivera do?
